dr_section_ref: DR §1 — Indonesian discourse particles (lah, sentence-final emphasis)
notes: Sentence-final 'lah' adds emphasis or mild impatience to a flat statement — 'Aku tahu' alone is neutral; 'Aku tahu lah' has a 'come on, obviously' undertone. English 'obviously' captures the impatient-emphasis register without being rude. VERIFY WITH GIRLFRIEND: 'duh' is more informal and arguably more accurate but can read as flippant — 'obviously' is safer. 'Of course' is another candidate that's gentler than 'obviously'.
